wikidata_candidates.label: Jerome Koch
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The two records describe individuals with significantly different professional backgrounds and locations. The card describes a Jerry Koch involved in machine design at M&T Chemicals in New Jersey, while the Wikidata entry describes a Jerome Koch who is a sociologist at Texas Tech University. These fields of expertise and career paths are highly distinct.

results.possibleIdentity: Jerry (Jerome) Koch was an engineer and technical professional active during the late 1960s and 1970s, specializing in machine design and chemical engineering. He was employed by M&T Chemicals (formerly Metal & Thermit Chemicals), which operated a major research and development facility in Rahway, New Jersey. Koch is documented as an engineer who collaborated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established to foster partnerships between artists and the technical community. His expertise in machine design was utilized to provide technical assistance to artists seeking to integrate mechanical systems into their works. In his professional capacity at M&T Chemicals, he was also an inventor, holding patents such as US Patent 3,745,183 (1973) for the preparation of organotin compounds. He appears to have graduated from the University of Michigan College of Engineering (Class of 1959).
